heading: Mineral security
label: 1606 Mineral security

A, title VIII, § 845135 Stat. 1842Dec. 27, 2021“The Secretary of Defense shall cooperate with the Secretary of Energy to ensure that the priorities of the Department of Defense with respect to the research and development of alternative technologies to, and methods for the extraction, processing, and recycling of, critical minerals (as defined in section 2(b) of the National Materials and Minerals Policy, Research, and Development Act of 1980 ()) are considered and included where feasible in the associated research and development activities funded by the Secretary of Energy pursuant to the program established under paragraph [probably should be “subsection”] (g) of section 7002 of division Z of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2021 () [].”30 U.S.C. 1601(b)Public Law 116–26030 U.S.C. 1606(g)

children.label: A
children.text: the recovery of which depends on the production of a host mineral that is not designated as a critical mineral; and

children.label: B
children.text: that exists in sufficient quantities to be recovered during processing or refining.

children.label: i
children.text: has a high risk of a supply chain disruption; and

children.label: ii
children.text: serves an essential function in 1 or more energy technologies, including technologies that produce, transmit, store, and conserve energy; or

children.label: B
children.text: a critical mineral.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “critical mineral” means any mineral, element, substance, or material designated as critical by the Secretary under subsection (c).

children.label: i
children.text: fuel minerals;

children.label: ii
children.text: water, ice, or snow;

children.label: iii
children.text: common varieties of sand, gravel, stone, pumice, cinders, and clay.

children.heading: Indian Tribe
children.label: 4 Indian Tribe
children.text: The term “Indian Tribe” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 5 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.label: A
children.text: a State;

children.label: B
children.text: the District of Columbia;

children.label: C
children.text: the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico;

children.label: D
children.text: Guam;

children.label: E
children.text: American Samoa;

children.label: F
children.text: the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands; and

children.label: G
children.text: the United States Virgin Islands.

children.label: A
children.text: an institution of higher education (as defined in ); orsection 1001(a) of title 20

children.label: B
children.text: a postsecondary vocational institution (as defined in ).section 1002(c) of title 20

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the draft methodology used to identify a draft list of critical minerals;

children.label: B
children.text: a draft list of minerals, elements, substances, and materials that qualify as critical minerals; and

children.label: C
children.text: a draft list of critical minerals recovered as byproducts and their host minerals.

children.heading: Availability of data
children.label: 2 Availability of data
children.text: If available data is insufficient to provide a quantitative basis for the methodology developed under this subsection, qualitative evidence may be used to the extent necessary.

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the final methodology for determining which minerals, elements, substances, and materials qualify as critical minerals;

children.label: B
children.text: the final list of critical minerals; and

children.label: C
children.text: the final list of critical minerals recovered as byproducts and their host minerals.

children.label: i
children.text: are essential to the economic or national security of the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: the supply chain of which is vulnerable to disruption (including restrictions associated with foreign political risk, abrupt demand growth, military conflict, violent unrest, anti-competitive or protectionist behaviors, and other risks throughout the supply chain); and

children.label: iii
children.text: serve an essential function in the manufacturing of a product (including energy technology-, defense-, currency-, agriculture-, consumer electronics-, and health care-related applications), the absence of which would have significant consequences for the economic or national security of the United States.

children.heading: Inclusions
children.label: B Inclusions
children.text: Notwithstanding the criteria under paragraph (3), the Secretary may designate and include on the list any mineral, element, substance, or material determined by another Federal agency to be strategic and critical to the defense or national security of the United States.

children.heading: Required consultation
children.label: C Required consultation
children.text: The Secretary shall consult with the Secretaries of Defense, Commerce, Agriculture, Health and Human Services, and Energy and the United States Trade Representative in designating minerals, elements, substances, and materials as critical under this paragraph.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the Secretaries of Defense, Commerce, Agriculture, Health and Human Services, and Energy and the United States Trade Representative, shall review the methodology and list under paragraph (3) and the designations under paragraph (4) at least every 3 years, or more frequently as the Secretary considers to be appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: revise the methodology described in this subsection;

children.label: ii
children.text: determine that minerals, elements, substances, and materials previously determined to be critical minerals are no longer critical minerals; and

children.label: iii
children.text: designate additional minerals, elements, substances, or materials as critical minerals.

children.heading: Notice
children.label: 6 Notice
children.text: On finalization of the methodology and the list under paragraph (3), or any revision to the methodology or list under paragraph (5), the Secretary shall submit to Congress written notice of the action.

children.label: A
children.text: identifies and quantifies known critical mineral resources, using all available public and private information and datasets, including exploration histories; and

children.label: B
children.text: provides a quantitative and qualitative assessment of undiscovered critical mineral resources throughout the United States, including probability estimates of tonnage and grade, using all available public and private information and datasets, including exploration histories.

children.heading: Supplementary information
children.label: 2 Supplementary information
children.text: In carrying out this subsection, the Secretary may carry out surveys and field work (including drilling, remote sensing, geophysical surveys, topographical and geological mapping, and geochemical sampling and analysis) to supplement existing information and datasets available for determining the existence of critical minerals in the United States.

children.heading: Public access
children.label: 3 Public access
children.text: Subject to applicable law, to the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall make all data and metadata collected from the comprehensive national assessment carried out under paragraph (1) publically and electronically accessible.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: 4 Technical assistance
children.text: At the request of the Governor of a State or the head of an Indian Tribe, the Secretary may provide technical assistance to State governments and Indian Tribes conducting critical mineral resource assessments on non-Federal land.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may sequence the completion of resource assessments for each critical mineral such that critical minerals considered to be most critical under the methodology established under subsection (c) are completed first.

children.label: i
children.text: identifies the sequence and schedule for completion of the assessments if the Secretary sequences the assessments; or

children.label: ii
children.text: describes the progress of the assessments if the Secretary does not sequence the assessments.

children.label: A
children.text: the generation of new information or datasets by the Federal Government; or

children.label: B
children.text: the receipt of new information or datasets from critical mineral producers, State geological surveys, academic institutions, trade associations, or other persons.

children.heading: Additional surveys
children.label: 7 Additional surveys
children.text: The Secretary shall complete a resource assessment for each additional mineral or element subsequently designated as a critical mineral under subsection (c)(5)(B) not later than 2 years after the designation of the mineral or element.

children.label: A
children.text: for which the United States was dependent on a foreign country for more than 25 percent of the United States supply, as depicted in the report issued by the United States Geological Survey entitled ‘Mineral Commodity Summaries 2021’; but

children.label: B
children.text: that is not designated as a critical mineral under subsection (c).

children.heading: Report of Small Business Administration
children.label: e Report of Small Business Administration

children.text: complying with chapter 6 of title 5 (commonly known as the “Regulatory Flexibility Act”), in promulgating regulations applicable to the critical minerals industry; and
performing an analysis of the efficiency of regulations applicable to the critical minerals industry, including those that are disproportionately burdensome to small businesses.

children.label: A
children.text: subject to any required reviews within the Department of the Interior or the Department of Agriculture; and

children.label: B
children.text: published in final form in the Federal Register not later than 45 days after the date of initial preparation of the notice.

children.heading: Preparation
children.label: 2 Preparation
children.text: The preparation of Federal Register notices required by law associated with the issuance of a critical mineral exploration or mine permit shall be delegated to the organizational level within the agency responsible for issuing the critical mineral exploration or mine permit.

children.label: A
children.text: the documents or meetings are held; or

children.label: B
children.text: the activity is initiated.

children.label: i
children.text: an exploration project in which the presence of a byproduct is reasonably expected, based on known mineral companionality, geologic formation, mineralogy, or other factors; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a project that demonstrates that a byproduct is of sufficient grade that, when combined with the production of a host mineral, the byproduct is economic to recover, as determined by the applicable Secretary in accordance with subparagraph (B), and that the byproduct will be recovered in commercial quantities.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: B Requirement
children.text: In making the determination under subparagraph (A)(ii), the applicable Secretary shall consider the cost effectiveness of the byproducts recovery.

children.heading: Recycling, innovation, efficiency, and alternatives
children.label: g Recycling, innovation, efficiency, and alternatives
A
children.text: to develop alternatives to critical materials that do not occur in significant abundance in the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: to promote the efficient production, use, and recycling of critical materials, with special consideration for domestic critical materials, throughout the supply chain;

children.label: C
children.text: to ensure the long-term, secure, and sustainable supply of critical materials; and

children.label: D
children.text: to prioritize work in areas that the private sector by itself is not likely to undertake due to financial or technical limitations.

children.label: A
children.text: Federal agencies, including the Department of the Interior;

children.label: B
children.text: the National Laboratories;

children.label: C
children.text: critical material producers, processors, and manufacturers;

children.label: D
children.text: trade associations;

children.label: E
children.text: academic institutions (including students and postdoctoral staff at institutions of higher education);

children.label: F
children.text: small businesses;

children.label: G
children.text: nongovernmental organizations; and

children.label: H
children.text: other relevant entities or individuals.

children.heading: Energy Innovation Hub
children.label: 3 Energy Innovation Hub
children.text: In carrying out the program, the Secretary may use an Energy Innovation Hub authorized under .section 18632 of title 42

children.label: A
children.text: alternative materials, particularly materials available in abundance within the United States and not subject to potential supply restrictions, that lessen the need for critical materials;

children.label: i
children.text: occur in abundance in the United States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: are not subject to potential supply restrictions;

children.label: C
children.text: technologies or process improvements that minimize the use and content, or lead to more efficient use, of critical materials across the full supply chain;

children.label: D
children.text: innovative technologies and practices to diversify commercially viable and sustainable domestic sources of critical materials, including technologies for recovery from waste streams;

children.label: E
children.text: technologies, process improvements, or design optimizations that facilitate the recycling of critical materials, and options for improving the rates of collection of products and scrap containing critical materials from post-consumer, industrial, or other waste streams;

children.label: i
children.text: efficient water and wastewater management strategies;

children.label: ii
children.text: technologies and management strategies to control the environmental impacts of radionuclides in ore tailings;

children.label: iii
children.text: technologies for separation and processing; and

children.label: iv
children.text: technologies for increasing the recovery rates of coproducts and byproducts from host metal ores;

children.label: G
children.text: commercial markets, advanced storage methods, energy applications, and other beneficial uses of critical materials; and

children.label: H
children.text: advanced theoretical, computational, and experimental tools necessary to support the crosscutting research and development needs of diverse critical minerals stakeholders.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall submit to Congress a plan to carry out the program.December 27, 2020

children.label: i
children.text: the research and development activities to be carried out under the program during the subsequent 2 years;

children.label: ii
children.text: the expected contributions under the program to the creation of innovative methods and technologies for the efficient and sustainable provision of critical materials to the domestic economy;

children.label: iii
children.text: the expected activities under the program to mitigate the environmental and health impacts of the extraction, processing, manufacturing, use, recovery, and recycling of critical materials; and

children.label: iv
children.text: how the program will promote the broadest possible participation by academic, industrial, and other contributors and the public.

children.heading: Coordination and nonduplication
children.label: 6 Coordination and nonduplication
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall ensure that the activities carried out under this subsection are coordinated with, and do not duplicate the efforts of, other programs within the Federal Government, including the work underway by the Critical Materials Institute and the National Minerals Information Center.

children.heading: Standard of review
children.label: 7 Standard of review
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary shall conduct a review of activities carried out under the program to determine the achievement of the technical milestones identified under paragraph (8)(D)(i)(I).December 27, 2020

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall establish and operate a Critical Materials Consortium (referred to in this paragraph as the “Consortium”) for the purpose of supporting the program by providing, to the maximum extent practicable, a centralized entity for multidisciplinary, collaborative, critical materials research and development.December 27, 2020

children.heading: Leadership
children.label: B Leadership
children.text: If an Energy Innovation Hub authorized under that is focused on critical materials exists on , the Secretary shall leverage the personnel and expertise of the Energy Innovation Hub to manage the Consortium for not less than 3 years following the date on which the Consortium is established.section 18632 of title 42December 27, 2020

children.heading: Membership
children.label: C Membership
children.text: The members of the Consortium shall be representatives from relevant Federal agencies, the National Laboratories, the National Minerals Information Center, institutions of higher education, private sector entities, multiinstitutional collaborations, and other appropriate entities.

children.label: I
children.text: identifies technical goals and milestones for the program;

children.label: II
children.text: utilizes the high performance computing capabilities of the Department; and

children.label: III
children.text: leverages the expertise of the National Laboratories and the United States Geological Survey; and

children.label: ii
children.text: submit an annual report to the Secretary summarizing the activities of the Consortium, including an evaluation of the role of the Consortium in the achievement of the technical milestones identified under clause (i)(I).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary may provide support to the Consortium for a period of not more than 10 years, subject to the availability of appropriations.

children.heading: Merit review
children.label: ii Merit review
children.text: Not later than 5 years after the date on which the Consortium is established, the Secretary shall conduct a rigorous merit review to determine whether the Consortium helped the program achieve the technical milestones identified under subparagraph (D)(i)(I).

children.heading: Termination
children.label: iii Termination
children.text: If the Secretary determines that the Consortium has not helped the program achieve the technical milestones identified under subparagraph (D)(i)(I), the Secretary may terminate any financial or technical support that the Department provides to the Consortium.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: 9 Reports
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report summarizing the activities, findings, and progress of the program.December 27, 2020

children.label: A
children.text: $125,000,000 for fiscal year 2021;

children.label: B
children.text: $105,000,000 for fiscal year 2022;

children.label: C
children.text: $100,000,000 for fiscal year 2023;

children.label: D

children.label: E
children.text: $135,000,000 for fiscal year 2025.

children.heading: Critical Materials Supply Chain Research Facility
children.label: h Critical Materials Supply Chain Research Facility

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary of Energy (referred to in this subsection as the “Secretary”) shall support construction of a Critical Materials Supply Chain Research Facility (referred to in this subsection as the “facility”).

children.label: A
children.text: shall be used to further enable research, development, demonstration, and commercialization activities throughout the supply chain for critical materials; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall provide an integrated, rapidly reconfigurable research platform.

children.label: A
children.text: $10,000,000 for fiscal year 2021;

children.label: B
children.text: $30,000,000 for fiscal year 2022; and

children.label: C
children.text: $35,000,000 for fiscal year 2023.

children.heading: Critical Materials Research Database and Information Portal
children.label: i Critical Materials Research Database and Information Portal

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In carrying out the program established under subsection (g)(1), the Secretary and the Secretary of Energy (referred to in this subsection as the “Secretaries”), in consultation with the Director of the National Science Foundation, shall establish and operate a Critical Materials Information Portal (referred to in this subsection as the “Portal”) to collect, catalogue, disseminate, and archive information on critical materials.

children.heading: Cooperation
children.label: 2 Cooperation
children.text: In carrying out paragraph (1), the Secretaries shall leverage the expertise of the National Minerals Information Center, the Office of Scientific and Technical Information, and the Critical Materials Consortium established under subsection (g)(8)(A).

children.label: A
children.text: to accelerate breakthroughs in critical materials identification and design;

children.label: B
children.text: to strengthen the foundation for technologies that will enable more sustainable recycling, substitution, use, and recovery and minimize the environmental impacts of methods for extraction, processing, and manufacturing of critical materials; and

children.label: C
children.text: to drive the development of advanced materials for applications that span the missions of the Department of Energy and the Department of the Interior (referred to in this subsection as the “Departments”) in energy, environment, and national security.

children.label: A
children.text: conduct cooperative research with industry, academia, and other research institutions to facilitate the design of novel materials, including critical materials and substitutes for critical materials;

children.label: B
children.text: leverage existing high-performance computing systems to conduct high throughput calculations and develop computing and data mining algorithms for the prediction of material properties, including a focus on critical materials;

children.label: C
children.text: leverage and support research in mineralogy and mineral chemistry to enhance the understanding, prediction, and manipulation of critical materials;

children.label: D
children.text: assist scientists and engineers in making the fullest possible use of the relevant data holdings of the Departments, including the scientific and technical data generated by the research and development activities funded under subsection (g);

children.label: E
children.text: seek and incorporate other information on critical materials to enhance the Departments’ utility for program participants and other users; and

children.label: F
children.text: manage and make available to researchers and the public accessible, curated, standardized, secure, and privacy-protected data sets from the public and private sectors for the purposes of critical materials research and development activities.

children.label: A
children.text: no person uses the information and data collected for the Portal for a purpose other than the development of, or reporting of, aggregate data in a manner such that the identity of the person or firm who supplied the information is not discernible and is not material to the intended uses of the information;

children.label: B
children.text: no person discloses any information or data collected for the Portal unless the information or data has been transformed into a statistical or aggregate form that does not allow the identification of the person or firm who supplied particular information; and

children.label: C
children.text: procedures are established to require the withholding of any information or data collected for the Portal if at least 1 of the Secretaries determines that the withholding is necessary to protect proprietary information, including any trade secrets or other confidential information.

children.label: i
children.text: the quantity of each critical mineral domestically produced during the preceding year;

children.label: ii
children.text: the quantity of each critical mineral domestically consumed during the preceding year;

children.label: iii
children.text: market price data or other price data for each critical mineral;

children.label: I
children.text: critical mineral requirements to meet the national security, energy, economic, industrial, technological, and other needs of the United States during the preceding year;

children.label: II
children.text: the reliance of the United States on foreign sources to meet those needs during the preceding year; and

children.label: III
children.text: the implications of any supply shortages, restrictions, or disruptions during the preceding year;

children.label: v
children.text: the quantity of each critical mineral domestically recycled during the preceding year;

children.label: vi
children.text: the market penetration during the preceding year of alternatives to each critical mineral;

children.label: vii
children.text: a discussion of international trends associated with the discovery, production, consumption, use, costs of production, prices, and recycling of each critical mineral as well as the development of alternatives to critical minerals; and

children.label: viii
children.text: such other data, analyses, and evaluations as the Secretary finds are necessary to achieve the purposes of this subsection; and

children.label: i
children.text: the quantity of each critical mineral projected to be domestically produced over the subsequent 1-year, 5-year, and 10-year periods;

children.label: ii
children.text: the quantity of each critical mineral projected to be domestically consumed over the subsequent 1-year, 5-year, and 10-year periods;

children.label: I
children.text: critical mineral requirements to meet projected national security, energy, economic, industrial, technological, and other needs of the United States;

children.label: II
children.text: the projected reliance of the United States on foreign sources to meet those needs; and

children.label: III
children.text: the projected implications of potential supply shortages, restrictions, or disruptions;

children.label: iv
children.text: the quantity of each critical mineral projected to be domestically recycled over the subsequent 1-year, 5-year, and 10-year periods;

children.label: v
children.text: the market penetration of alternatives to each critical mineral projected to take place over the subsequent 1-year, 5-year, and 10-year periods;

children.label: vi
children.text: a discussion of reasonably foreseeable international trends associated with the discovery, production, consumption, use, costs of production, and recycling of each critical mineral as well as the development of alternatives to critical minerals; and

children.label: vii
children.text: such other projections relating to each critical mineral as the Secretary determines to be necessary to achieve the purposes of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: no person uses the information and data collected for the report for a purpose other than the development of or reporting of aggregate data in a manner such that the identity of the person or firm who supplied the information is not discernible and is not material to the intended uses of the information;

children.label: B
children.text: no person discloses any information or data collected for the report unless the information or data has been transformed into a statistical or aggregate form that does not allow the identification of the person or firm who supplied particular information; and

children.label: C
children.text: procedures are established to require the withholding of any information or data collected for the report if the Secretary determines that withholding is necessary to protect proprietary information, including any trade secrets or other confidential information.

children.label: A
children.text: skills that are in the shortest supply as of the date of the assessment;

children.label: B
children.text: skills that are projected to be in short supply in the future;

children.label: C
children.text: the demographics of the critical minerals industry and how the demographics will evolve under the influence of factors such as an aging workforce;

children.label: D
children.text: the effectiveness of training and education programs in addressing skills shortages;

children.label: E
children.text: opportunities to hire locally for new and existing critical mineral activities;

children.label: F
children.text: the sufficiency of personnel within relevant areas of the Federal Government for achieving the policies described in ; andsection 1602 of this title

children.label: G
children.text: the potential need for new training programs to have a measurable effect on the supply of trained workers in the critical minerals industry.

children.label: i
children.text: to design an interdisciplinary program on critical minerals that will support the critical mineral supply chain and improve the ability of the United States to increase domestic, critical mineral exploration, development, production, manufacturing, research, including fundamental research into alternatives, and recycling;

children.label: ii
children.text: to address undergraduate and graduate education, especially to assist in the development of graduate level programs of research and instruction that lead to advanced degrees with an emphasis on the critical mineral supply chain or other positions that will increase domestic, critical mineral exploration, development, production, manufacturing, research, including fundamental research into alternatives, and recycling;

children.label: iii
children.text: to develop guidelines for proposals from institutions of higher education with substantial capabilities in the required disciplines for activities to improve the critical mineral supply chain and advance the capacity of the United States to increase domestic, critical mineral exploration, research, development, production, manufacturing, and recycling; and

children.label: iv
children.text: to outline criteria for evaluating performance and recommendations for the amount of funding that will be necessary to establish and carry out the program described in paragraph (3).

children.heading: Report
children.label: B Report
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary shall submit to Congress a description of the results of the study required under subparagraph (A).December 27, 2020

children.label: i
children.text: startup costs for newly designated faculty positions in integrated critical mineral education, research, innovation, training, and workforce development programs consistent with paragraph (2);

children.label: ii
children.text: internships, scholarships, and fellowships for students enrolled in programs related to critical minerals;

children.label: iii
children.text: equipment necessary for integrated critical mineral innovation, training, and workforce development programs; and

children.label: iv
children.text: research of critical minerals and their applications, particularly concerning the manufacture of critical components vital to national security.

children.heading: Renewal
children.label: B Renewal
children.text: A grant under this paragraph shall be renewable for up to 2 additional 3-year terms based on performance criteria outlined under paragraph (2)(A)(iv).

children.label: i
children.text: the matter under the heading “geological survey” of the first section of the Act of (); orMarch 3, 187943 U.S.C. 31(a)

children.label: ii
children.text: the first section of ().Public Law 87–62643 U.S.C. 31(b)

children.heading: Effect on Department of Defense
children.label: B Effect on Department of Defense
children.text: Nothing in this section or an amendment made by this section affects the authority of the Secretary of Defense with respect to the work of the Department of Defense on critical material supplies in furtherance of the national defense mission of the Department of Defense.

children.heading: Secretarial order not affected
children.label: C Secretarial order not affected
children.text: This section shall not apply to any mineral described in Secretarial Order No. 3324, issued by the Secretary on , in any area to which the order applies.December 3, 2012

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: o Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this section $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2029.
